description:
Replace the typer's single-FnTypes-per-FnIr model with per-callsite specialization keyed by `(FnId, input-Descr-tuple)`. Each unique input signature gets its own typed body, its own Cranelift FuncId, its own Schema. The lub-aggregation that widens self-recursive returns to `any` disappears; monomorphic flows (the common case) get monomorphic ABIs end-to-end.

## Motivation

fz's typer originally joined all callsites' arg Descrs into one Descr per param, then computed one return Descr. For a self-recursive fn like `fact`, the fixed point widened to `any` because the lub didn't narrow. This forced workarounds:

- `ir_typer.rs:148-151` — explicit "leave cont slot 0 at `any`" hack
- `ir_typer.rs:166-168` — closure-reachability skip
- The natively_callable cascade existed in part to handle conts with `any` captures

All symptoms of the wrong typer key. Per-callsite specialization eliminated them.

## What specialization does

For each Call site, the typer instantiates the callee's body with the actual arg Descrs at that site. Different callsites produce different (FnTypes, return Descr, Schema, Signature, compiled body). Recursive calls converge via fixed point over (fn, key) pairs (same shape as today's fixed point, finer key).

Result: `fact(int)` returns `int`. fact's body is monomorphically int → goes native end-to-end. Cont frames disappear from typed flows. The dispatch fallbacks that exist for `any` flows still exist for genuinely dynamic paths (closures, message passing) but fire vastly less often.

## Arc body (all shipped)

- `.29.1` — Re-key ModuleTypes; fixed point over (FnId, key); no codegen behavior change yet.
- `.29.2` — SpecId indirection + 8 keyed-structure conversion + per-spec compile_fn.
- `.29.7` — Per-callsite narrow-spec consumption (the .29.2.1 atom).
- `.29.3` — Closures/Spawn/Receive fall back to the any-key specialization.
- `.29.4` — Kill the workarounds; lift the cont slot-0 pin; refresh closure-target tests.

## Closing piece (open)

- `.29.5` — **Closure-as-stub-and-captures.** Compile-time per-shape stubs replace the runtime closure-invoke cluster. Spawn-with-captures restriction lifts. Schema-driven GC. Subsumes the originally-scoped .29.5 (closure-callsite flow) and .29.6 (runtime kind-aware copy) entirely. See ticket for full design.

## Follow-up (out of arc)

- `.29.5.1` — Lift `used_as_closure_target` from the `natively_callable` exclusion. Possible once stubs serve as ABI adapters into native bodies.

## Supersedes

- `fz-ul4.27.5.4.1` — closed as superseded; the multi-callsite agreement problem doesn't exist under specialization.
- `fz-ul4.27.5.6` — entirely subsumed; part A's "closure flow narrowing" framing replaced by .29.5's stub design.

## CHILDREN

- [x] fz-ul4.29.1 P2 .29.1: Re-key ModuleTypes to (FnId, input-key)
- [x] fz-ul4.29.2 P2 .29.2: SpecId indirection + per-spec codegen
- [x] fz-ul4.29.7 P2 .29.2.1: Per-callsite narrow-spec consumption
- [x] fz-ul4.29.3 P2 .29.3: Closure/Spawn/Receive any-key fallback
- [x] fz-ul4.29.4 P2 .29.4: Kill workarounds, update tests
- [ ] fz-ul4.29.5 P2 .29.5: Closure-as-stub-and-captures
- [ ] fz-ul4.29.8 P3 .29.5.1: Lift closure-target native-eligibility (post-arc)
title: [EPIC] Per-callsite typer specialization
